title: [BUG] Column headers/keys not preserved when writing to a file?
author: srinidhisrinivas

comments.author: Alanscut
comments.body:
@sarahdmsi See [Microsoft documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/documentformat.openxml.spreadsheet.column?view=openxml-2.8.1) . The `column` tag does not contain the `key` attribute, The generated `XML` file will not contain the `key` attribute, so we cannot use the `key` to access it after re reading the file.

Personally, we probably need to remove the ability to access the information of the column through the `key`. what do you think @guyonroche @alubbe @Siemienik?
```xml
  // sheet1.xml
  <cols>
    <col min="1" max="1" width="10" customWidth="1"/>
    <col min="2" max="2" width="32" customWidth="1"/>
    <col min="3" max="3" width="10" customWidth="1"/>
  </cols>
```

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
1. should works when:
```js
worksheet2.columns = [
		{ header: 'Id', key: 'id', width: 10 },
		{ header: 'Name', key: 'name', width: 32 },
		{ header: 'D.O.B.', key: 'dob', width: 10 }
	];
```

2. It should works well if we add key into XML (probably others than exceljs just ignore additional info). -yeah! I know it just eccentric idea:).

3. exceljs uses `key` in some other ways, so we should not remove it. It will also break the backward compability.

3.

comments.author: yanickrochon
comments.body:
I also came here about this; I'm creating workeheets with headers and keys, and was hoping to find that information back when loading the file again. But everything is undefined. Why does this feature exist if it isn't supported by the file format?

What are the workarounds? Is there some way to store metadata in Excel? I would guess so because Microsoft is well known to save user data in `.docx` and `.xlsx` (among other file formats)!!
